title: carpinus caroliniana subsp. caroliniana
intro: Carpinus caroliniana subsp. caroliniana, also known as the American hornbeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Eastern North America. Traditionally, the bark and roots of the American hornbeam have been used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating anxiety, likely due to its flavonoid and phenolic content. Herbal preparations of American hornbeam often involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from the bark and roots, which can be used to support overall well-being and address specific health concerns.
